one_line_definition: Generation and/or detection of ultrasonic waves using pulsed or CW lasers, without physical contact with the specimen.
Laser pulse intensity low enough that thermal expansion (not ablation) launches elastic waves; non-destructive; principal stresses parallel to surface.
Laser intensity high enough to vaporise surface material, producing a reactive normal stress and a broad compressional lobe; slight surface damage occurs.
Elastic wave confined to the near-surface region, with displacement decaying exponentially with depth; wavelength determines penetration depth.
Variation of SAW phase velocity with frequency in layered or graded media, enabling depth-profiling of elastic properties.
Material with spatially varying composition and microstructure producing continuously changing properties, often modelled as a stack of homogeneous layers.
Formula relating effective composite elastic modulus to constituent moduli and volume fractions; MROMs adds an empirical stress-strain transfer coefficient Q.
Recursive matrix technique linking stress and displacement fields across layers; used to solve multilayer wave propagation problems analytically.
Optimisation procedure that fits measured SAW dispersion data to a forward model to recover depth-dependent material properties.
Pump-probe optical technique using femtosecond/picosecond laser pulses to generate and detect acoustic pulses in the GHz–THz range, enabling nanometre thin-film measurements.
Acoustic phonon dispersion relation in a periodic multilayer (Bloch waves); at low frequency the effective velocity depends on layer velocities and impedances.
Group velocity of acoustic pulses propagating in a periodic multilayer at the long-wavelength limit, determined by the Rytov dispersion formula.
Product of material density and sound velocity; mismatch between layers determines wave reflection and transmission at interfaces.

aliases_seen: time delay estimation; cross-correlation velocity measurement
one_line_definition: Signal processing technique matching two consecutive echo waveforms to measure round-trip propagation time with precision below the digitiser sampling interval.
Systematic velocity overestimate caused by the finite source size in pulse-echo measurement; derived differently for laser (dipolar/normal stress) vs. piston sources.
Variation of phase velocity with frequency in scattering or absorbing media; measured by frequency-domain cross-correlation of echoes.

aliases_seen: Lame constants; Young's modulus; Poisson's ratio; C11, C12, C44
one_line_definition: Second-order stiffness tensor components relating stress and strain; fully characterise linear elastic response of isotropic or anisotropic solids.
Non-contact transducer using Lorentz and magnetostrictive forces to generate/detect ultrasound in electrically conducting materials without couplant; sensitive to lift-off.
Ultrasonic generation or detection using air as the coupling medium; enables non-contact inspection but suffers from large impedance mismatch with solids.
Optical technique recording interference between a reference beam and light reflected from a vibrating surface; provides full-field images of ultrasonic wavefronts.
Use of a patterned transmission mask to illuminate the test surface with a periodic intensity pattern, generating narrowband toneburst surface waves at a selectable frequency.

aliases_seen: horizontal force pair; centre of expansion
one_line_definition: Simplified equivalent source for thermoelastic laser generation: two equal and opposite horizontal forces at the surface, producing characteristic oblique-lobe directivity for shear and longitudinal waves.
Characteristic equation whose real root kR gives the Rayleigh wave wavenumber; poles of the Green's function integral correspond to Rayleigh wave excitation.
Complex wavenumber of the thermal diffusion wave launched in the solid by laser heating; kT >> kl,t at NDT frequencies, making the thermal penetration depth very small.
Angular dependence of acoustic amplitude radiated from a source; for laser generation differs fundamentally from piezoelectric transducers due to the thermoelastic source geometry.
Two-beam optical interferometer used to measure surface displacement with sub-nanometre resolution; phase-locking stabilises against low-frequency vibration.
Ultrasonic technique measuring arrival time of waves diffracted from crack tips to size and locate defects; benefits from the short broadband pulses of laser generation.
